_note: CANDIDATE WNY seed batch, converted from listings.json by convert.mjs. NOT reviewed, NOT imported. Every record needs source sign-off and the _review flags resolved before import (see CONVERSION-NOTES.md). All claims import as self_reported (§4).
source: WNY seed research batch 2 2026-07-09 (Erie County beachhead, §3)

listings.name: ACCES-VR Buffalo District Office (NYS Education Department)
listings.summary: The NYS Education Department's ACCES-VR Buffalo District Office provides vocational rehabilitation services including counseling, employment preparation, business services, and transition/youth services for people with disabilities.

_review.source_notes: First-party NYS Education Department page (fetched OK). Page lists 'Vocational Rehabilitation Services,' 'Transition and Youth Services,' and 'Business Services'; states 'Interpreter and ASL services available' and notes the nearest rapid transit stop (Fountain Plaza) is half a block north — communication-access and transit info only, no citable physical-access attribute statements (step-free/restroom/parking), so attributes stay empty. State agency, not disabled-led. TTY line published.

listings.name: Beyond Support Network (merger of Cantalician Center for Learning and Learning Disabilities Association of WNY)
listings.summary: Formed by the August 2022 merger of the Cantalician Center for Learning (est. 1956) and the Learning Disabilities Association of WNY (est. 1965), Beyond Support Network serves 1,200+ people with learning and developmental disabilities annually with school-age, preschool, day habilitation, residential, employment, and arts (Starlight Studio) programs.

_review.source_notes: First-party history page is the source but beyondwny.org returned HTTP 403 to fetching — details from search excerpts of that page plus the Cantalician-hosted announcement PDF: 'In August of 2022, the Cantalician Center for Learning and Learning Disabilities Association of WNY completed a merger... officially forming Beyond Support Network.' Re-verify on the live site. This single record replaces both pre-merger names (LDA of WNY and Cantalician Center) — list the merged entity. Campus/admin address 2049 George Urban Blvd, Depew (Erie County) per cantaliciancenter.org; zip not confirmed, left null. Legacy LDA mission wording: 'to provide high-quality individualized, comprehensive, and innovative services, which support, educate, and empower all individuals with learning and/or developmental disabilities.' No disabled-led basis; no physical-access statements.

listings.name: Deaf Access Services (a program of People Inc.)
listings.summary: Founded in 1983, Deaf Access Services provides sign language interpreting, CART, ASL classes, employment (Career Pathways via ACCES-VR), and peer-to-peer assistance for Deaf, hard of hearing, DeafBlind, and hearing people across the eight counties of Western New York.

_review.source_notes: First-party People Inc. program page (fetched OK). Exact wording: DAS exists to 'promote communication access, awareness and opportunities in the community'; programs include 'Peer-to-Peer Assistance for the Deaf and Hard of Hearing.' DAS became a program of People Inc. (formerly independent, est. 1983; renamed from Deaf Adult Services in 2014). No explicit Deaf-run/peer-run statement on the current page, so disabled_led=false despite the org's Deaf-community roots — flagged for confirmation at onboarding. Address caveat: the People Inc. page shows the corporate 1219 North Forest Rd, Williamsville address; the Tri-Main Center Suite 446 address is from the 211WNY directory entry ('Tri-Main Center 2495 Main Street - Suite 446, Buffalo, New York 14214-2155') — reconfirm suite at onboarding. No physical-access statements published.

listings.name: Gliding Stars Adaptive Ice Skating (Gliding Stars of WNY)
listings.summary: Gliding Stars of WNY is an adaptive ice skating program (October–March, ending in a public ice show) that provides individuals with disabilities instruction and practice using adaptive skates and walkers, headquartered in Buffalo with sessions in West Seneca and Niagara Falls.

_review.source_notes: First-party program page (fetched OK). Exact mission wording: 'provides individuals with disabilities the opportunity to increase their personal potential through development of ice skating skills in a regular program of instruction and practice.' National HQ at 1307 Military Rd, Buffalo 14217 (Erie County). Program sites: Southtowns location in West Seneca (Erie) and Hyde Park Ice Arena, Niagara Falls (outside Erie — county coverage is mixed but HQ and one site are Erie). No leadership statement; no physical-access statements published. Chosen over Miracle League of WNY, which was not verified with a first-party page in this batch.

listings.name: Headway of Western New York, Inc.
listings.summary: Established in 1985, Headway of WNY supports people with brain injuries, other cognitive conditions, and seniors through information, education, referrals, TBI/NHTD waiver service coordination, and peer and caregiver support groups across six WNY counties.

_review.source_notes: First-party site (fetched OK). Exact mission wording: 'Established in 1985, Headway of WNY is a non-profit agency whose mission is to ensure that individuals in Western New York with brain injuries, other cognitive conditions, and seniors have access to resources and support systems to enable them to live as independently as possible.' 211WNY lists it as an affiliate of People Inc. Runs TBI survivor PEER support groups, but no peer-run/consumer-controlled governance statement, so disabled_led=false. Address on current site is 2495 Main St Suite 446 (older org PDF shows a prior 2635 Delaware Ave address — Tri-Main is current; reconfirm suite since DAS lists the same one). No physical-access statements published.

listings.name: New York State Commission for the Blind (NYSCB) — Buffalo District Office
listings.summary: The NYS Commission for the Blind's Buffalo district office (NYS Office of Children and Family Services) provides vocational rehabilitation and independent-living services to people who are legally blind.
listings.street: Ellicott Square Building, 295 Main St, Room 545

_review.source_notes: First-party OCFS district-offices page is the source, but ocfs.ny.gov refused the fetch (connection reset, treated as fetch-blocked) — address confirmed via the search excerpt of that same page: 'Ellicott Square Building, 295 Main Street, Room 545, Buffalo, NY 14203, phone 716-931-3500.' Re-verify against the live page. State agency serving legally blind New Yorkers; not disabled-led; no physical-access statements captured.

listings.name: Parent Network of WNY
listings.summary: Parent Network of WNY provides one-on-one support, workshops, resources, and support groups for families of individuals with disabilities (birth through adulthood) and for professionals, serving about 10,000 people a year.

_review.source_notes: First-party about page (fetched OK). Exact mission: 'Supporting families and professionals to empower individuals with disabilities to reach their full potential.' Site states 'The majority of Parent Network of WNY's staff and board members are parents of children with disabilities' — that is family-led, not disabled-led, so disabled_led=false per the citable-basis rule. Spanish-language support line offered. No physical-access statements published.

listings.name: SABAH, Inc.
listings.summary: Formed in 1977 as the Skating Association for the Blind and Handicapped, SABAH provides adaptive ice skating, adaptive ice hockey, and year-round therapeutic recreation and fitness programs for more than 650 participants with physical, cognitive, and/or emotional disabilities.

_review.source_notes: First-party about page (fetched OK). Exact mission wording: 'Enriching the quality of life for individuals challenged by physical, cognitive, and/or emotional disability by providing education and therapeutic recreation, promoting fitness, and developing social and communication skills.' Office at 2607 Niagara St, Buffalo 14207; programs run at area rinks/turf sites. 211WNY renders the acronym as 'Spirited Athletes Bold at Heart (SABAH)'; the site itself uses simply 'SABAH Inc.' No leadership-by-disabled-people statement; no physical-access statements published.

listings.name: Self-Advocacy Association of New York State (SANYS) — Western Region
listings.summary: The SANYS Western (Buffalo) regional office supports self-advocacy by people with developmental disabilities through regional meetings, conferences, trainings, and support for self-advocacy groups across seven WNY counties including Erie.

_review.source_notes: First-party regional page (fetched OK) plus sanys.org homepage. disabled_led basis (exact wording from sanys.org): SANYS is 'an organization founded by and led by people with developmental disabilities for people with developmental disabilities'; site tagline: 'People with developmental disabilities, speaking up for ourselves and others.' Strong citable peer-run basis — NEEDS SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING. Regional office address from the SANYS site (1021 Broadway, 4th Floor — same building as Parent Network); a 211WNY entry shows an alternate 1200 East and West Rd, Bldg 16, West Seneca address — reconfirm current office. No physical-access statements published.

listings.name: BestSelf Behavioral Health Clinic — Franklin Street
listings.summary: A downtown Buffalo outpatient behavioral-health clinic offering adult and child/adolescent psychiatry, substance-use disorder treatment, and case management, with 211WNY listing accessible building, parking, restrooms, and visual emergency alarms.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' plus 'Building accessible, parking, visual emergency alarms and restrooms.' Mapping decisions: restrooms and parking asserted (explicit); entrance_step_free NOT asserted because 'building accessible' is a general claim that does not specify a step-free entrance. NOTE: 'visual emergency alarms' is Deaf/HoH-relevant evidence with no schema attribute slot — held as a note. Other BestSelf sites show similar 211 accessibility descriptions (Delaware Ave and PROS list 'telecommunication for the deaf'; Orchard Park lists 'interpreter for the deaf'; East Buffalo is listed NOT accessible) — each site would need its own record. disability_literate held FALSE conservatively: large general behavioral-health org; the deaf-accommodation line items are facility accommodations, not a stated disability-competence program. Recruitment candidate given the unusually specific 211 accessibility detail.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Building accessible, parking, visual emergency alarms and restrooms' — restrooms explicitly listed in the accessibility description.
211WNY directory: 'Building accessible, parking, visual emergency alarms and restrooms' — parking explicitly listed in the accessibility description.

listings.name: Buffalo Hearing & Speech Center
listings.summary: A Buffalo nonprofit providing pediatric and adult audiology, hearing aid and cochlear implant services, speech-language therapy, and oral deaf education programs.

_review.source_notes: First-party site (askbhsc.org Buffalo location page, fetched 2026-07-09) plus 211WNY (https://www.211wny.org/provider/3566/ and program page /provider/6064/). disability_literate TRUE on explicit first-party competence: 'Comprehensive Hearing Evaluations for Children and Adults,' 'Cochlear Implant Program,' 'Listening & Spoken Language Program' for children with hearing loss; 211WNY describes the 'Oral Deaf Education Program [which] services children who are deaf or hard of hearing utilizing a listening and spoken language approach.' NO physical-access attributes asserted: neither the first-party page nor the 211 org page publishes an accessibility statement (first-party page notes only that the location is 'easily accessible by public transportation, including the Metro Rail' — a transit statement, not a physical-access claim). Recruitment note: Deaf/HoH-competence anchor for the WNY seed; field-verify entrance/restroom/parking.

listings.name: Buffalo VA Medical Center (VA Western New York Healthcare System)
listings.summary: A federal VA hospital serving veterans with full medical, surgical, and emergency care plus disability-specific programs including blind and low-vision rehabilitation, audiology and cochlear implant management, and spinal cord injury care.

_review.source_notes: Two sources fetched 2026-07-09: first-party va.gov facility page (high reliability) and 211WNY (https://www.211wny.org/provider/6918/, 'Handicap Accessible? Yes'). va.gov exact wording: 'Wheelchairs are available upon arrival for patients who need them to access the building' — a service statement, not a step-free-entrance claim, so the single conservative attribute rides on the 211 general flag. disability_literate TRUE on explicit first-party programs: Blind and Low Vision Rehabilitation (visual skills assessments, adaptive aids training), Audiology (hearing aids, cochlear implant management), Spinal Cord Injuries and Disorders (coordinated lifetime care), Physical Medicine & Rehabilitation. Eligibility limited to veterans/active-duty — note for directory UX. HONEST FLAG NOTE: mde_deadline_recruit_title_ii deliberately NOT applied — this is a FEDERAL facility governed by the Architectural Barriers Act and Section 504, not ADA Title II, so the Aug 9 2026 Title II MDE deadline does not apply.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention; re-verify the entrance specifically. va.gov adds: 'Wheelchairs are available upon arrival for patients who need them to access the building.'

listings.name: Community Health Center of Buffalo (CHCB)
listings.summary: A Buffalo FQHC providing primary medical, dental, OB/GYN, pediatric, pharmacy, and social-work services in over 30 languages, listed as handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' (general flag only). NOTE: the Buffalo site is at 34 Benwood Ave (14214), not Main St as sometimes assumed. FQHC status corroborated by chcb.net search excerpt ('Federally Qualified Health Center operating patient centers across Western New York in Erie and Niagara Counties') and nhchc.org grantee directory — chcb.net itself returned HTTP 403 on fetch, hence source_fetch_403_reverify. HHS-funded, past July 8 2026 MDE deadline — recruit for exam-table/scale photo verification. No disability-specific competence program stated (language access in 30+ languages is notable but not disability competence) — disability_literate false.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention; re-verify the entrance specifically.

listings.name: DENT Neurologic Institute — Amherst (DENT Tower)
listings.summary: A large outpatient neurology practice in Amherst whose Neuromuscular Disorders Center hosts the Muscular Dystrophy Association (MDA) Clinic and provides multidisciplinary ALS and neuromuscular care.

_review.source_notes: First-party page fetched 2026-07-09 (high reliability), corroborated by the MDA care-center directory (https://www.mda.org/clinic/dent-neurologic-institute, same 3980 Sheridan Drive address). Exact first-party wording: 'We work closely with the Muscular Dystrophy Association (MDA), hosting the MDA Clinic at both our Amherst and Orchard Park locations'; the center 'specializes in treating the entire spectrum of neuromuscular disorders, including ALS..., peripheral neuropathy, myopathy, myasthenia gravis, and muscular dystrophy,' with clinic physicians and therapists 'targeting the specific needs of neuromuscular conditions.' disability_literate TRUE on this explicit disability-specific clinic model (MDA Care Center + monthly ALS support group at DENT Tower). NO physical-access attributes: neither dentinstitute.com nor a 211WNY listing publishes an accessibility statement for this site (the practice is on the 2nd floor per third-party listings — elevator presumed but NOT asserted). Field-verify entrance/restroom/parking; strong recruit given its mobility-disability patient population.

listings.name: Jericho Road Community Health Center — Barton Street Clinic
listings.summary: A Buffalo FQHC clinic on the West Side offering family medicine, pediatrics, OB/GYN, behavioral health, and pharmacy services, listed as handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY (United Way) directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' (general flag only; no entrance/restroom/parking breakdown). Services listed include family and community medicine, pediatrics, OB/GYN, general psychiatry, diabetes management, pharmacy. FQHC (Jericho Road CHC) — HHS-funded, past the July 8 2026 MDE deadline; strong recruit to photo-verify exam table/scale. Refugee/immigrant-focused primary care, no disability-specific competence program stated — disability_literate false. Corroborated address via jrchc.org search results (184 Barton St, 716-881-6191).

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention; re-verify the entrance specifically.

listings.name: Jericho Road Community Health Center — Broadway Clinic
listings.summary: A Buffalo FQHC clinic on Broadway (3rd floor) offering primary care, OB/GYN, pediatrics, dental, behavioral health, and physical therapy, whose 211WNY listing explicitly states it is NOT handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. IMPORTANT NEGATIVE: exact wording is 'Handicap Accessible? No.' Do NOT present this site as accessible; zero attributes asserted. Included for directory coverage and as a priority recruitment/re-verification target: the clinic is on Floor 3 of 1021 Broadway, and as an FQHC site it is HHS-funded and past the July 8 2026 MDE deadline — the 'No' flag may be stale or building-specific and should be field-verified. Services per 211: routine/family care, maternity/OBGYN, pediatrics, dental, behavioral health, physical therapy, pharmacy consultation, interpretation.

listings.name: Neighborhood Health Center — Mattina
listings.summary: A Buffalo FQHC site on Niagara Street offering family medicine, dental (including emergency and pediatric), OB/GYN, podiatry, and substance-use treatment, listed as handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' (general flag only). Same org as batch-1's NHC Northwest and Blasdell records; this Mattina site (300 Niagara St) was not in batch 1. FQHC — HHS-funded, past July 8 2026 MDE deadline; recruit for MDE photo verification. Serves medically under-insured, uninsured, and people experiencing homelessness; no disability-specific competence program stated — disability_literate false. First-party corroboration: nhcwny.org/locations-and-hours/location:mattina/.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention; re-verify the entrance specifically.

listings.name: Neighborhood Health Center — Southtowns
listings.summary: A Hamburg (Southtowns) FQHC site offering community-clinic primary care, OB/GYN, medical social work, nutrition services, and health education, listed as handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' (general flag only). Address is the relocated Southtowns office at 151 Elmview Avenue, Hamburg (corroborated by search results on the NHC relocation). FQHC — HHS-funded, past July 8 2026 MDE deadline; recruit for MDE photo verification. No disability-specific competence program stated — disability_literate false.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes.' A GENERAL flag, not an entrance-specific claim — mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention; re-verify the entrance specifically.

listings.name: Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center
listings.summary: A Buffalo comprehensive cancer center (NYS public-benefit corporation) whose 211WNY listing states it has ramps, elevators, accessible public restrooms, and wheelchair access.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' and 'Ramps, elevators, and accessible public restrooms. Accessible to wheelchairs.' Mapping: only accessible_restroom asserted; 'ramps' and 'accessible to wheelchairs' do NOT specify a step-free entrance or designated accessible parking, so those were not inferred. Roswell also runs interpreter/language-assistance services and has an accessibility problem-report form (forms.roswellpark.org/accessibility-issue-report), but no first-party physical-access page was verified — roswellpark.org accessibility claims found only via search excerpts. Title II flag caveat: Roswell Park is a New York State public-benefit corporation, so the Aug 9 2026 ADA Title II MDE deadline likely applies — confirm entity classification during recruitment. Generic (cancer) hospital — disability_literate false despite support groups and disease/disability information services.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Ramps, elevators, and accessible public restrooms. Accessible to wheelchairs.' — accessible public restrooms explicitly stated.

listings.name: The Summit Center
listings.summary: A large Amherst-area nonprofit serving children and adults with autism and other developmental or behavioral disabilities through evaluations, an early autism program, behavioral pediatrics and feeding clinics, and Summit Academy.

_review.source_notes: 211WNY directory page, fetched 2026-07-09 — medium reliability. Exact wording: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' AND 'Call for accessibility' (the latter lowers confidence — prioritize field re-verification). disability_literate TRUE: explicitly a disability-specific provider — 211/first-party (thesummitcenter.org) describe autism and eligibility evaluations, Early Autism Program (birth-to-five EIBI), Behavioral Pediatrics Clinic, Pediatric Feeding Clinic, Summit Academy (ages 3-21 with autism/developmental disabilities), and adult developmental services. Address is 150 Stahl Road, Getzville 14068 (Town of Amherst, Erie County). Not an FQHC and not a public entity — no MDE-deadline flag. Strong co-design/recruitment partner candidate for cognitive-access testing.

attributes.note: 211WNY directory: 'Handicap Accessible? Yes' — a GENERAL flag mapped conservatively per batch-1 convention. CAUTION: the same listing also says 'Call for accessibility,' so treat this as weaker than other general flags and re-verify the entrance specifically.

listings.name: NFTA Paratransit Access Line (PAL)
listings.summary: NFTA-Metro's ADA paratransit service providing shared-ride, advance-reservation, origin-to-destination accessible transportation for riders whose disability prevents use of fixed-route bus or rail.

_review.source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). Exact wording: 'PAL is NFTA-Metro's ADA paratransit service. It is a shared-ride, advanced reservation service for individuals who are eligible under ADA paratransit rules'; 'Accessible vehicles are used for all PAL trips'; operators 'can assist a rider, when needed, to and from the vehicle and the outermost door of a building.' Companion page https://metro.nfta.com/paratransit/how-pal-works details origin-to-destination assistance (steadying arm, ramp/lift deployment, wheelchair securement) and the December 7, 2025 extended-service-hours pilot ('PAL trips are available in the core service area whenever a Metro bus or rail is in service'). disability_literate=true is supported by the service being ADA-paratransit-specific with published disability-assistance practices; disabled_owned/disabled_led=false (public authority, no such claim). Facility attributes (exam table/scale/parking) not applicable to a transit service, so none asserted. Reservations (716) 855-7239; eligibility application required. NEEDS first-person rider experience reports for community validation.

listings.name: Buffalo-Depew Station (Amtrak)
listings.summary: Amtrak's suburban Buffalo station in Depew, served by Empire Service, Maple Leaf, and Lake Shore Limited trains.

_review.source_notes: Great American Stations is Amtrak's own station-information site (first-party; fetched 2026-07-09). Address 55 Dick Road, Depew, NY 14043 (Erie County). Exact wording: 'Accessible platform,' 'Accessible restrooms,' 'Accessible ticket office,' 'Accessible waiting room,' 'Wheelchair lift available,' and free same-day/overnight accessible parking. entrance_step_free NOT asserted — platform boarding relies on a wheelchair lift ('Wheelchair lift available') and the source does not explicitly state a step-free entrance. NEEDS on-site confirmation of entrance path and lift operability.

attributes.note: Amtrak's Great American Stations page lists 'Accessible restrooms' at this station.
Amtrak's page states 'Same-day, accessible parking is available for free' and 'Overnight, accessible parking is available for free.'

listings.name: Buffalo Exchange Street Station (Amtrak)
listings.summary: Downtown Buffalo's Amtrak station, rebuilt as a new ADA-compliant facility that opened in November 2020, served by Empire Service and Maple Leaf trains.

_review.source_notes: Great American Stations is Amtrak's own station-information site (first-party; fetched 2026-07-09). Address 75 Exchange Street, Buffalo, NY 14203. Exact wording: 'Accessible platform,' 'High platform,' 'Accessible waiting room,' 'Accessible restrooms,' 'Accessible ticket office,' 'Accessible water fountain,' 'Wheelchair available,' 'No wheelchair lift,' plus same-day and overnight 'accessible parking is available; fees may apply.' entrance_step_free NOT asserted — the source describes an accessible high-level platform but never explicitly states a step-free building entrance. amtrak.com/stations/bfx timed out during verification; station page exists per Amtrak site search results. NEEDS on-site confirmation of step-free entrance path from street to waiting room and platform.

attributes.note: Amtrak's Great American Stations page lists 'Accessible restrooms' at this station.
Amtrak's page states 'Same-day, accessible parking is available; fees may apply' and 'Overnight, accessible parking is available; fees may apply.'

listings.name: Buffalo Niagara International Airport
listings.summary: The NFTA-operated commercial airport for the Buffalo region, with a published accessibility program covering wheelchair assistance, companion care restrooms, accessible parking, Aira visual interpreting, and the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower Program.

_review.source_notes: First-party NFTA/airport accessibility page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl; the site's TLS chain trips some fetch tools but content was retrieved and verified). Exact wording quoted in attribute notes; page also covers wheelchair assistance (pre-arrange at 716-633-3726 or on arrival), Braille signage 'posted throughout the airport,' Video Relay Service units, electric carts, curbside handicap pickup spaces at posts #5 and #10, Aira free on site, autism acclimation sessions, and 'All airport shuttles are equipped with a wheelchair lift.' Address from site footer: 4200 Genesee Street, Buffalo, NY 14225 (facility physically sits in Cheektowaga, Erie County — city given as on the official site). entrance_step_free NOT asserted (not explicitly stated). NEEDS on-site confirmation of terminal entrance details.

attributes.note: Airport's own accessibility page: 'Companion Care Restrooms are provided throughout the terminal and are large enough to accommodate wheelchairs and families,' with pre- and post-security locations listed.

Airport's own accessibility page: 'Free handicapped parking is available for up to 59 minutes in the Daily/Hourly Garage' and 'All lots have handicapped spaces available for those with a valid handicap parking permit.'

listings.name: Allen-Medical Campus Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line serving Allentown and the Buffalo Niagara Medical Campus.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09; note NFTA sites have a TLS chain issue for some fetch tools but content retrieved and verified via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities' and 'Wheelchair riders traveling in the subsurface (underground) portion of the system may board any railcar.' Same page describes elevator-breakdown procedures ('In the event of an elevator breakdown, wheelchair users may use the PACE panel to request assistance'), so underground-station access is elevator-dependent — no entrance attribute asserted. Station listed on the current route page (https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail, schedule effective 06/21/2026) as ALLEN-MEDICAL CAMPUS. Underground/subsurface configuration corroborated by Wikipedia (Buffalo Metro Rail). NEEDS street address, on-site first-person confirmation of elevator path, and current elevator status check before any attribute claim.

listings.name: Amherst Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line at the Amherst Street area of Buffalo.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities'; elevator-dependent underground station, so no entrance attribute asserted. Listed as AMHERST on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (effective 06/21/2026; nearby landmark Tri-Main Center). NEEDS street address, per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Church Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Surface-level NFTA Metro Rail station on Main Street at Church Street in downtown Buffalo.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency route page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl; schedule effective 06/21/2026). CAUTION — active construction and altered accessible boarding: service alert states 'Starting Sunday, June 7, Metro Rail will be single tracking, above ground only, from Church to Canalside Stations... When boarding at Church, Seneca, and Canalside stations, use the outbound platform (toward University). The inbound platforms are closed.' Accessibility note states riders using mobility devices boarding underground and traveling to Church/Seneca/Canalside must 'board the rear car of the train. This will ensure your train car opens to an elevated platform at the station,' and riders from Fountain Plaza/Lafayette must transfer at Allen or use the Route 8 bus. The system-wide accessibility page (https://metro.nfta.com/special-services/accessibility) says surface-station wheelchair boarding normally uses 'the first door of the first railcar... to have access to boarding platforms on the Buffalo Place Mall.' City of Buffalo 'Cars on Main Street' construction is scheduled along Main between Church and Exchange Streets summer 2026 (https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/rail-renewal/). No per-station attribute asserted. NEEDS re-verification after single-tracking/construction ends and on-site confirmation of accessible boarding path.

listings.name: Delavan Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line serving the Delavan Avenue area and Canisius University.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities'; elevator-dependent underground station, so no entrance attribute asserted. Listed as DELAVAN on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (effective 06/21/2026; route landmarks include Canisius University); historically named Delavan/Canisius College — verify current signage. NFTA Rail Renewal page (https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/rail-renewal/) notes May 2026 underground single-tracking track work 'began near the Delavan platform' — service pattern may be disrupted. NEEDS street address, per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: DL&W Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: NFTA Metro Rail's newest station, opened December 8, 2025 inside the historic DL&W trainshed at the foot of Main Street near Canalside and KeyBank Center.

_review.source_notes: First-party NFTA announcement dated 12/8/2025: 'the DL&W Metro station is now open to the public'; corroborated by the current route page (https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail), which lists DL&W as the downtown terminus and states 'DL&W Station will be open' during June 2026 single-tracking. NFTA capital-improvement page (https://www.nfta.com/about/capital-improvement/dlw-station) says the project addresses 'current accessibility challenges in the areas surrounding the trainshed, fostering a more inclusive environment for all users, including individuals with disabilities' but names no specific station access features, so no attribute asserted. Ongoing work: pedestrian skybridge to KeyBank Center and 2nd-floor remediation 'expected completion in 2026,' and Michigan Avenue entrance / Riverwalk access improvements still advancing — surroundings are an active project area. Station entrance is off South Park Avenue at the Shoreline Trail side per the capital page; exact street address not published. NEEDS street address, explicit statement of step-free/elevator access, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Humboldt-Hospital Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line serving the Sisters of Charity Hospital area.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities'; elevator-dependent underground station, so no entrance attribute asserted. Listed as HUMBOLDT-HOSPITAL on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (effective 06/21/2026; landmarks include Sisters of Charity Hospital). Rail Renewal page notes outbound-track rail replacement 'between Humboldt-Hospital and Allen-Medical Campus' currently underway — check service alerts. NEEDS street address, per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: LaSalle Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line serving the LaSalle Avenue area, one stop south of University Station.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities'; elevator-dependent underground station, so no entrance attribute asserted. Listed as LASALLE on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (effective 06/21/2026). NEEDS street address (a park-and-ride lot is associated with this station — verify accessible parking on site), per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Seneca Station - Merchants Insurance (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Surface-level NFTA Metro Rail station on Main Street near Seneca Street in downtown Buffalo, close to Sahlen Field.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency route page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl; schedule effective 06/21/2026) lists the station as 'SENECA STATION - MERCHANTS INSURANCE' (sponsored name). CAUTION — same June 2026 single-tracking alert as Church Station: 'When boarding at Church, Seneca, and Canalside stations, use the outbound platform (toward University). The inbound platforms are closed,' with mobility-device riders directed to board the rear car from underground stations or transfer at Allen from Fountain Plaza/Lafayette. System-wide claim 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible' (https://metro.nfta.com/special-services/accessibility) is not per-station. No attribute asserted. NEEDS re-verification after single-tracking ends and on-site confirmation of the accessible boarding platform.

listings.name: Summer-Best Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line between the Summer Street and Best Street area of Buffalo.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities.' Underground station — access depends on elevators per the same page's elevator-breakdown guidance, so no entrance attribute asserted. Station listed as SUMMER-BEST on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (schedule effective 06/21/2026); underground configuration corroborated by Wikipedia. NEEDS street address, per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Utica Station (NFTA Metro Rail)
listings.summary: Underground NFTA Metro Rail station on the Main Street line at the Utica Street area of Buffalo.

_review.source_notes: First-party transit-agency page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl). System-wide statement only: 'All Metro Rail trains and stations are accessible to riders with disabilities'; underground stations are elevator-dependent per the page's elevator-breakdown procedure, so no entrance attribute asserted. Listed as UTICA on https://metro.nfta.com/schedules/route/rail (effective 06/21/2026); underground configuration corroborated by Wikipedia. NEEDS street address, per-station elevator confirmation, and on-site first-person confirmation.

listings.name: NFTA Metropolitan Transportation Center
listings.summary: NFTA's downtown Buffalo bus terminal and headquarters at 181 Ellicott Street, serving Metro bus routes and intercity carriers, with the walk-in Metro Customer Care center.

_review.source_notes: First-party NFTA-Metro page (fetched 2026-07-09 via curl): 'Visit our Customer Care center at the Metropolitan Transportation Center (Bus Station) at 181 Ellicott St.' The NFTA Accessibility Advisory Committee also meets 'in person at 181 Ellicott Street, Buffalo, New York 14203' per https://metro.nfta.com/special-services/accessibility, and the ADA/EEO Administrator is based there. NFTA publishes no specific facility accessibility features (entrance, restroom, parking) for the terminal itself, so no attributes asserted. NEEDS on-site confirmation of entrance, restroom, and parking accessibility.

listings.name: Buffalo and Erie County Botanical Gardens
listings.summary: Historic 1899 conservatory and botanical garden stating most areas including restrooms are ADA-compliant, with designated accessible parking and loaner wheelchairs.

_review.source_notes: First-party 'Amenities and accessibility' page (fetched 2026-07-09); high reliability. Caution wording quoted exactly: 'Due to the historic nature of the conservatory, some areas may present challenges to those with ambulatory disabilities; however, most areas, including restrooms, are accessible and ADA-compliant.' Loaner wheelchairs first-come first-served at the admissions desk (no schema key). An expansion project (per buffalogardens.com/about/our-expansion/) includes a new accessible entrance with drop-off circle — in progress, so entrance conditions may change; entrance_step_free NOT asserted (never stated explicitly). Adapted guided tours offered; aides/personal care attendants admitted free.

attributes.note: Source states the Gardens have 'accessible restrooms, drinking fountains and water bottle filling stations for visitors to use during their visit' and that 'most areas, including restrooms, are accessible and ADA-compliant.'
Source states: 'Designated parking spots for people with disabilities are available in our parking lot. A passenger drop-off zone can also be utilized at the main entrance circle.'

listings.name: Buffalo and Erie County Naval & Military Park
listings.summary: Waterfront museum of decommissioned naval vessels whose own FAQ states that handicap access aboard the historic ships is extremely limited.

_review.source_notes: First-party FAQ page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact wording: 'We regret that due to the historical nature of the ships, handicap access is extremely limited.' Visitors with accessibility questions are directed to info@buffalonavalpark.org or 716-847-1773 ext. 18. The main visit pages carry no accessibility statement at all. Third-party sources (nyfalls.com, Wheel the World) describe ship weather decks as reachable but interior/below-decks compartments as not wheelchair accessible, with many ladder stairs; the indoor museum building and shop are reportedly accessible — none of this is first-party-confirmed, so NO attributes are asserted. Listed for coverage/honesty: this venue's own statement is the model of the caution this record should convey.

listings.name: Buffalo Zoo
listings.summary: AZA-accredited zoo in Delaware Park offering free wheelchair rentals and KultureCity sensory bags at admissions.

_review.source_notes: First-party Visitor Info page (fetched 2026-07-09). States 'Wheelchair - Free of charge' rentals (photo ID or deposit required; phone reservations for motorized scooters/wheelchairs), and free sensory bags: 'Bags include: noise-reducing headphones, sunglasses, fidgets, visual cue cards, and a KultureCity VIP lanyard.' Certified service dogs welcome ('harnessed, leashed, or tethered'; excluded from Giraffe House and Rainforest Falls). The page makes NO explicit statement about a step-free entrance, accessible restrooms, or accessible parking, so no schema attributes are asserted. A dedicated /accessibility/ URL returns 404; the zoo is a KultureCity partner with a social story (per search results). Wheelchair rental and sensory offerings have no schema key — described here only.

listings.name: Burchfield Penney Art Center
listings.summary: Art museum at Buffalo State University committed to ADA accommodation, with loaner wheelchairs, a T-coil hearing loop in its auditorium, and programs for visitors with memory loss.

_review.source_notes: First-party Visit page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact wording: 'Visitors needing to request accommodation under ADA should call 716-878-6011, or email burchfld@buffalostate.edu'; 'Wheelchairs are available and can be reserved in advance by calling (716) 878-6011 during gallery hours'; 'The Burchfield's Auditorium has a loop system (T-coil) for loop-enabled hearing aids.' Search excerpts add 'the galleries and art spaces are wheelchair accessible' and that the museum 'is committed to the letter and intent of the Americans with Disabilities Act,' but the fetched page carries no explicit step-free-entrance, accessible-restroom, or accessible-parking statement, so no schema attributes are asserted. Parking in Buffalo State Lot R-3 directly in front (free weekends) — accessible spaces not explicitly described. Hearing loop and loaner wheelchairs have no schema key — noted here only.

listings.name: Frank Lloyd Wright's Graycliff
listings.summary: Frank Lloyd Wright lakeside estate whose historic house is stated by the site itself as not currently accessible, though the visitor pavilion is accessible and two designated accessible parking spots exist.

_review.source_notes: First-party Plan Your Visit page (fetched 2026-07-09); high reliability. Exact wording: 'As a historic house, Graycliff is not currently accessible due to a variety of reasons, including stairs, unpaved and uneven areas, lighting limitations, and other concerns' and 'Our Visitor Pavilion is accessible at this time.' Mobility assistance is via a golf cart ('first come first serve reservation', call (716) 947-9217) that is 'weather dependent... not available if it is raining, or has recently rained, or during the off-season of November through March' — significant reliability caveat, no schema key. Search results indicate the Standard tour first floor is described as wheelchair accessible on tour pages, but the main house's overall non-accessibility statement governs; no entrance/restroom attributes asserted. Wheelchairs not furnished; visitors' own mobility devices allowed with rubber wheels/tips.

attributes.note: Source states: 'A parking area is located just outside the M&T Bank Visitor Pavilion with two designated accessible parking spots.'

listings.name: Frank Lloyd Wright's Martin House
listings.summary: Frank Lloyd Wright historic house museum whose standard 75-minute tour is mostly wheelchair accessible except the pergola and 2nd floor, with an accessible visitor center and one designated parking spot.

_review.source_notes: First-party dedicated accessibility page (fetched 2026-07-09); high reliability. Exact wording: 'The majority of the Martin House 75-minute tour is wheelchair accessible, with the exception of the pergola'; per FAQs, also excepting the 2nd floor ('Walkers are not allowed on the 2nd floor of the Martin House'). 'Our Greatbatch Pavilion Visitor Center and Museum Store are accessible, as are many of the outdoor areas.' The Martin House Plus tour is explicitly NOT wheelchair accessible; Barton House and Gardener's Cottage are NOT wheelchair accessible (per martinhouse.org rental/FAQ pages). Page warns of 'a considerable amount of walking, standing, and stair-climbing' on most tours with 'very few places to sit down.' No explicit statement on step-free entrance or accessible restrooms — not asserted. Only ONE accessible parking spot — capacity caveat.

attributes.note: Source states: 'There is a handicapped parking spot behind the Greatbatch Pavilion' (a single spot; call (716) 856-3858 in advance about availability).

listings.name: KeyBank Center
listings.summary: 19,000-seat downtown arena (Sabres/Bandits) stating all entrances are accessible with power doors, accessible seating on all levels, accessible restrooms, elevators with Braille and audible tones, and accessible parking in its ramp.
listings.street: One Seymour H. Knox III Plaza

_review.source_notes: First-party accessibility page (Buffalo Bandits site, same Pegula Sports & Entertainment operator as the arena; fetched 2026-07-09) — mirrors keybankcenter.com/page/ada-policy, which exists but failed direct fetch on a TLS/header error, not a content problem. Exact wording: 'All entrances in the KeyBank Center are accessible. The main entrances to the building are equipped with electric power doors'; 'Accessible seats are available on all Levels of the KeyBank Center. Companion seats are also available for each reserved seat' (up to three companions); elevators 'equipped with Braille signs and audible tones.' Entrance asserted as 'accessible,' not literally 'step-free,' so entrance_step_free is NOT asserted. Sensory kits and assisted listening devices free at Guest Services (deposit required) — KultureCity sensory-certified venue per third-party coverage; no schema key, noted here only.

attributes.note: Source states: 'Restrooms are located throughout the concourses. All restrooms are accessible and have baby-changing tables,' plus unisex family restrooms on the 100, 200, and 300 levels.
Source states accessible parking spaces for guests with disabilities are 'located throughout the facility on all Levels next to the elevators' in the KeyBank Center parking ramp.

listings.name: Kleinhans Music Hall
listings.summary: Historic concert hall and home of the Buffalo Philharmonic Orchestra with main-floor wheelchair seating, an accessible lobby-level restroom, and accessible parking.

_review.source_notes: First-party dedicated accessibility page (fetched 2026-07-09); high reliability. Exact wording: 'Automatic doors are located at both entrances as well as the accessible restroom on the lobby level'; 'Wheelchair-accessible seating is located on the main floor. Please note, there is no elevator to the balcony level.' Caution: balcony level is NOT reachable by elevator (an elevator serves lobby-to-lower-level only, near the Pennsylvania Ave. entrance). Entrance not asserted as step-free because the page says 'automatic doors,' not step-free. Assistive listening ('augmentative hearing devices') and noise-muffling headphones at coat check have no schema key — noted here only. Box Office (716) 885-5000 for accessible seating.

attributes.note: Source states: 'There is a private, accessible restroom on the lobby level. Accessible stalls are also available in the general restrooms on the upper and lower levels.'
Source states: 'Accessible parking is available on a first come, first served basis.' Drop-off available on the Pennsylvania side of the building.

listings.name: Sahlen Field (Buffalo Bisons)
listings.summary: Downtown Triple-A ballpark whose official A-Z guide describes accessible seating rows, elevators on the first-base side, and restrooms equipped to serve disabled patrons.

_review.source_notes: First-party Ballpark A-Z Guide on MiLB.com (official Bisons site). One fetch tool was blocked (HTTP 406) during research, but the page was verified directly the same day (2026-07-09) with a standard browser user agent — exact wording confirmed: 'Restrooms: All restrooms are equipped and accessible to serve all patrons.'; 'Accessible Seating: There are reserved accessible seat locations on the Main level'; 'only one escort is allowed to accompany fans using accessible seating' (capacity caveat); recognized service/guide/hearing dogs allowed; loaner wheelchairs via fan services. No explicit step-free-entrance or accessible-parking statement, so those attributes are not asserted.

attributes.note: Guide states: 'Restrooms: All restrooms are equipped and accessible to serve all patrons,' and Family Restrooms at Sections 115 and 116 on the main concourse are each 'able to serve physically challenged patrons.' Wording verified on the live page 2026-07-09.

listings.name: Theatre of Youth (Allendale Theatre)
listings.summary: Children's theater in the historic 1913 Allendale Theatre with 10 wheelchair locations in the house, wheelchair-accessible stalls in both lobby restrooms, and sensory-friendly performances.

_review.source_notes: First-party pages (fetched 2026-07-09). The auditions page carries the most explicit facility statement: 'The Allendale Theatre offers seating for 445 people and 10 wheelchair locations' and the restroom wording quoted in the attribute; it also states 'our stage and rehearsal room are currently not accessible as a result of steps or stairs' (affects performers/participants, not audience) 'seeing as it is a city building.' Ticket Policies page (theatreofyouth.org/ticket-policies/) adds: 'Please inform the box office, when ordering tickets, if a wheelchair location or hearing-assisted device is required.' Sensory-friendly performances offered (theatreofyouth.org/sensory-friendly/) — no schema key. Historic building; no explicit step-free-entrance or accessible-parking statement, so those are not asserted. Odd source page (auditions) but first-party and specific — a dedicated accessibility page would be a better long-term citation.

attributes.note: Source states: 'there is a female and male identifying set of bathrooms in our lobby with each respective restroom having one wheelchair accessible stall.'

listings.name: Akron Falls Park
listings.summary: Erie County Heritage Park in Akron whose county accessibility page lists it among the Heritage Parks that received accessible playgrounds by Summer 2023.

_review.source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks 'Accessibility & Inclusion' page (fetched 2026-07-09). The page lists Akron Falls Park among the Heritage Parks that received accessible playgrounds (completed by Summer 2023). An accessible playground has no schema attribute key, so it is described here only; the page does not state accessible parking or an accessible restroom specifically for Akron Falls Park, so no attributes are asserted.

listings.name: Bennett Beach
listings.summary: Erie County lakefront beach park in Angola (Town of Evans) where the county installed beach matting from the bridge out to the beach.

_review.source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks 'Accessibility & Inclusion' page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact wording: 'beach matting at Bennett Beach park from the bridge out to the beach'. Beach access matting has no schema attribute key, so it is described in summary only; the page does not state accessible parking or an accessible restroom for Bennett Beach, so no attributes are asserted.

listings.name: Buffalo Harbor State Park
listings.summary: NYS state park on Buffalo's Outer Harbor whose 2026 renovation, per a May 2026 Governor's press release, includes an expanded playground with universally accessible elements plus a new sprayground, cafe, and public restrooms.

_review.source_notes: First-party NYS Governor's press release (May 21, 2026), announcing the sprayground and completed park improvements as open. Exact accessibility wording: 'An expanded playground including universally accessible elements, featuring swinging, spinning and zip-line elements'. An accessible playground has no schema attribute key. The release mentions 'public restrooms' in the new cafe building but does NOT call them accessible, and does not state accessible parking, so no attributes are asserted. The parks.ny.gov park page itself (https://parks.ny.gov/parks/buffaloharbor and /details) contains no explicit accessibility section; third-party sources claim a paved harbor path and accessible pier but were not used. Suggest on-site confirmation for restroom/parking attributes.

listings.name: Canalside
listings.summary: Public waterfront destination on Buffalo's Inner Harbor whose official site states that all of its parking lots offer accessible parking and publishes a dedicated ADA parking/accessibility map.

_review.source_notes: First-party buffalowaterfront.com Canalside page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact wording: 'All lots offer accessible parking'; the site also links a 'Buffalo-Waterfront-ADA-Parking' accessibility map PDF (https://buffalowaterfront.com/u/pdf/Buffalo-Waterfront-ADA-Parking.pdf) and notes Metro Rail access (Erie Canal Harbor stop). Wheelchair-accessible restrooms in the Longshed/Chandlery buildings are claimed on a separate Buffalo History Museum-managed exhibit page, not on this page, so an accessible_restroom attribute is NOT asserted here.

attributes.note: Official Canalside page states: 'All lots offer accessible parking' and 'Accessible parking is also available inside the Harborcenter parking ramp at 100 Washington Street if Canalside parking is not available.'

listings.name: Como Lake Park
listings.summary: Erie County Heritage Park in Lancaster whose county accessibility page lists it among the Heritage Parks that received accessible playgrounds by Summer 2023.

_review.source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks 'Accessibility & Inclusion' page (fetched 2026-07-09). The page lists Como Lake Park among the Heritage Parks that received accessible playgrounds (completed by Summer 2023). Playgrounds have no schema attribute key; the page does not state accessible parking or an accessible restroom specifically for Como Lake Park, so no attributes are asserted.

listings.name: Harlem Road Fishing and Boating Access (Buffalo Blueway)
listings.summary: NYSDEC/Buffalo Niagara Waterkeeper Buffalo Blueway site on the Buffalo River that reopened in April 2026 with an ADA-accessible pedestrian walkway, a new fishing deck platform, and an improved paddle craft launch.

_review.source_notes: First-party NYSDEC press release (May 8, 2026) confirming the site is reopened (April 2026 ribbon cutting with Buffalo Niagara Waterkeeper). Exact wording: 'Americans with Disabilities Act accessible pedestrian walkway and new fishing deck platform, a staircase with railings, natural shoreline stabilization, and improvements to the paddle craft launch.' Walkway, fishing deck, and paddle launch have no schema attribute keys, so they are described here only. The release does not state ADA parking or restrooms, so no attributes are asserted. Regional coverage of the same project (Buffalo Toronto Public Media, 2026-04-14) describes it as the region's second ADA-accessible fishing pier.

listings.name: Isle View Park
listings.summary: Erie County waterfront park in Tonawanda along the Niagara River where the county accessibility page states an accessible playground opened in Fall 2025.

_review.source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks 'Accessibility & Inclusion' page (fetched 2026-07-09): accessible playground at Isle View Park 'opened Fall 2025' (already operational as of 2026-07). Playgrounds have no schema attribute key, so no attributes are asserted; the page does not state accessible parking or an accessible restroom for this park.

listings.name: Ralph Wilson Park
listings.summary: Buffalo waterfront park undergoing a phased $110M reconstruction; as of mid-2026 the skate park, a temporary dog park, and a football field are open while the core park remains under construction.

_review.source_notes: First-party Ralph Wilson Park Conservancy 'Park Updates' page (fetched 2026-07-09). Page states the skate park is 'Open now!', the core park is under construction, the splash pad/pool are 'Closed for the season, but will reopen again next summer', and the kayak area and play area are 'Future phase'; 'Construction is well underway on the shoreline'. News coverage (WKBW; Buffalo Toronto Public Media, June 2026) reports Phase One (bridge, Fourth Street Park, Great Lawn, fields) targeted for summer 2026 with full Phase One opening now expected in 2027 and Phase Two running into 2027-2028. Per safety rules, NO access attributes are asserted for an in-progress project; re-verify after Phase One formally opens.

listings.name: Sprague Brook Park
listings.summary: Erie County park in Glenwood (Town of Concord) where the county accessibility page states an accessible playground opened in Fall 2024.

_review.source_notes: First-party Erie County Parks 'Accessibility & Inclusion' page (fetched 2026-07-09): accessible playground at Sprague Brook Park 'opened Fall 2024'. Playgrounds have no schema attribute key, so no attributes are asserted; the page does not state accessible parking or an accessible restroom for this park. The page also says all Erie County Parks rentable shelters will include universal picnic tables — a program-wide statement, not asserted per park.

listings.name: City of Tonawanda Public Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the City of Tonawanda in northern Erie County.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair Accessible Parking Lot and Building.' The general_accessible_flag_only flag applies to the entrance_step_free claim (derived from the building-level 'Wheelchair Accessible ... Building' phrase); the accessible_parking claim is explicit. No restroom detail given.

attributes.note: Page states 'Wheelchair Accessible Parking Lot and Building.' The building claim is a general accessible flag, not an explicit step-free-entrance statement. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.
Page explicitly states 'Wheelchair Accessible Parking Lot' — an explicit accessible-parking statement, not a bare 'parking lot'.

listings.name: Grand Island Memorial Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the Town of Grand Island in northwestern Erie County.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair accessible. Parking lot available.' Bare 'parking lot' not mapped to accessible_parking (no accessible/handicap-parking statement). No restroom or entrance detail given. Page listed summer hours (May 26–Sept 7) at fetch time.

attributes.note: Branch page states only 'Wheelchair accessible.' — a general building-level flag, not an explicit statement about a step-free entrance. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Julia Boyer Reinstein Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the Town of Cheektowaga, located on Losson Road between Union Road and Borden Road.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair accessible.' plus 'Parking lot available.' Bare 'parking lot' not mapped to accessible_parking (no accessible/handicap-parking statement). No restroom or entrance detail given.

attributes.note: Branch page states only 'Wheelchair accessible.' — a general building-level flag, not an explicit statement about a step-free entrance. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Lackawanna Public Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the City of Lackawanna, located on Ridge Road between Route 5 and South Park Avenue.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair accessible. Parking lot available.' Bare 'parking lot' not mapped to accessible_parking (no accessible/handicap-parking statement). No restroom or entrance detail given.

attributes.note: Branch page states only 'Wheelchair accessible.' — a general building-level flag, not an explicit statement about a step-free entrance. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Orchard Park Public Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the Town of Orchard Park in southern Erie County.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair accessible. Parking lot available.' A bare 'parking lot' is NOT mapped to accessible_parking — no statement that accessible/handicap spaces exist. No restroom or entrance detail given.

attributes.note: Branch page states only 'Wheelchair accessible.' — a general building-level flag, not an explicit statement about a step-free entrance. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.

listings.name: West Seneca Public Library
listings.summary: Buffalo & Erie County Public Library system branch serving the Town of West Seneca, with public meeting and study rooms.

_review.source_notes: First-party branch page (fetched 2026-07-09). Exact accessibility wording: 'Wheelchair accessible. Parking lot available.' Bare 'parking lot' not mapped to accessible_parking (no accessible/handicap-parking statement). No restroom or entrance detail given.

attributes.note: Branch page states only 'Wheelchair accessible.' — a general building-level flag, not an explicit statement about a step-free entrance. Conservative mapping; needs first-person confirmation.

listings.name: Buffalo Niagara Convention Center
listings.summary: Downtown Buffalo convention venue with a dedicated first-party ADA accessibility page describing wheelchair-accessible bathrooms, ramps, elevators, Braille signage, and wheelchair loans.

_review.source_notes: First-party ADA Accessibility page, fetched 2026-07-09. Exact wording: 'Bathrooms throughout BCC are wheelchair accessible.'; 'Elevators and wheelchair ramps for the physically challenged are located on the Marquee Level.'; 'Braille plates are available throughout the facility for the visually impaired.'; limited wheelchair loans at the Information Desk. The ramps/elevator statement describes internal circulation, not the entrance, so no entrance_step_free claim; the page gives no accessible-parking details, so no parking claim.

attributes.note: First-party ADA page states: 'Bathrooms throughout BCC are wheelchair accessible.'

listings.name: Buffalo RiverWorks
listings.summary: Large waterfront entertainment complex (events, sports, brewery, watersports) whose own site states its kayak launch is fully handicap accessible.

_review.source_notes: First-party watersports page, fetched 2026-07-09, states exactly: 'Kayak launch is fully handicap accessible.' This facility-specific statement does not map to any allowed attribute key (entrance/restroom/parking), so the record carries no attribute claims — coverage only. No general accessibility page found on buffaloriverworks.com; Wheel the World has a RiverWorks accessibility profile but the page is JS-rendered and could not be fetched for quotable data.

listings.name: Greek to Me Restaurant
listings.summary: Family Greek diner in Tonawanda (est. 2002) whose owning LLC is a New York State-certified Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Business.

_review.source_notes: Official NYS OGS SDVOB certified-business directory (public search app; record retrieved 2026-07-09 from its public API, control number 221379): legal name 'Greek to Me Restaurant, LLC', description 'Food Service', countyLocatedIn 'Erie', NAICS 722511 (full-service restaurants). SDVOB certification requires >=51% ownership AND day-to-day control by a service-disabled veteran — hence both disabled_owned and disabled_led, but this is the veteran subset only. Directory physical address on file is 107 Calvin Court South, Tonawanda (appears to be a registered/home address); the operating restaurant is at 2309 Eggert Rd Ste 9, Tonawanda per the restaurant's public listings (used for address confirmation only, NOT for any accessibility data). Restaurant's own site (greektomebuffalo.com) was unreachable at research time. No first-party accessibility statements found, so zero attribute claims. NEEDS OWNER SELF-ATTESTATION AT ONBOARDING.

listings.name: Pearl Street Grill & Brewery
listings.summary: Multi-floor downtown Buffalo brewpub; Visit Buffalo's accessibility guide describes staff providing a ramp for the one-step entrance and an elevator in the back between floors.

_review.source_notes: Visit Buffalo Niagara (regional tourism bureau) 'Accessibility Guide to Buffalo' page, fetched 2026-07-09 — a third-party editorial visit account, medium reliability. It states staff provided 'a one-step ramp' and that there is an 'elevator in the back to use to move between floors.' The entrance evidently has a step (ramp deployed on request), so entrance_step_free is NOT claimed and the elevator/ramp statements map to no allowed attribute key — coverage only, with partial-accessibility caution. No first-party accessibility statement found on pearlstreetgrill.com.

listings.name: Resurgence Brewing Company (Chicago Street taproom)
listings.summary: Brewery and taproom in Buffalo's Old First Ward whose own site notes an elevator off the main lobby serving the second-floor event and dining space.

_review.source_notes: First-party taproom page, fetched 2026-07-09, states exactly: 'an elevator is also available just to the left when you come into the main lobby.' An elevator statement maps to none of the three allowed attribute keys, so no attribute claims. Page mentions a 'FREE Parking Lot' but does not say any spaces are designated accessible, and it makes no entrance or restroom accessibility statements. (A search snippet describing a ground-level main entrance and a wheelchair lift to the patio traces to TripAdvisor UGC and was NOT used.) Note: Resurgence's former 1250 Niagara St location is closed; this record is the operating 55 Chicago St site.

listings.name: West Side Bazaar
listings.summary: Small-business incubator marketplace operated by WEDI, housing international food vendors and retail shops on Buffalo's West Side.

_review.source_notes: Tourism Cares Meaningful Travel Map listing with a description supplied by the organization itself (WEDI/West Side Bazaar): 'We offer tours and we are wheelchair accessible with ramps and an elevator.' WEDI's own site (wedibuffalo.org/westside-bazaar) and the Visit Buffalo listing page contain NO explicit accessibility statements — the accessible-building/parking/restroom claims that appear in search snippets could not be traced to a fetchable first-party page, so only the quoted ramps-and-elevator statement is used. Restroom and parking accessibility left unclaimed.

attributes.note: Business-supplied description on the Tourism Cares Meaningful Travel Map states: 'We offer tours and we are wheelchair accessible with ramps and an elevator.' General wheelchair-access statement mentioning ramps — conservatively mapped to a single entrance claim; not a literal 'step-free entrance' statement.
